concepts.title: Equation of ellipse
concepts.conceptBody:
Ellipse 

The ellipse is a locus of a point which moves such that ratio of its distances from a fixed point (called focus) and from a fixed line (called directrix) is always constant. This constant (denoted by e) is called eccentricity of the ellipse which must be less than 1. Alternatively the locus of a point which moves such that sum of its distances from two fixed points is always constant is called ellipse. We shall later see that the fixed points are two foci of the ellipse and the constant is equal to length of the major axis of the ellipse.

For every ellipse there is a point called center of the ellipse through which every chord of the ellipse is bisected. The line joining centre and focus of the ellipse is called major axis of the ellipse. A line perpendicular to the major axis and passing through the centre of the ellipse is called minor axis of the ellipse. The distance between the points on the ellipse which lie on major axis is called length of the major axis. The length of the minor axis of an ellipse is distance between two points on its which lie on minor axis. If the major and minor axis are along x and y-axis respectively with lengths 2a and 2b and centre of the ellipse be origin the equation of the ellipse can be proved to be

$\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$

Deriving the equation of the ellipse in the standard form : 

Let us choose a vertical line $V{{\text{ }}^{\prime }}V$as directrix and S as focus. Draw SZ $\bot V{{\text{ }}^{\prime }}V.$ Let $e<1$ eccentricity of the ellipse. There must be a point A on SZ such that

SA = e*AZ ...(i)

 

There must be another point ${{A}^{\prime }}\,(since\,\,\text{e}<1)$

On AS produced such that $\text{S}{{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\,\,=\,\,e\,\text{ }\,{{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\text{Z}$ ...(ii)

$Let\text{ }{{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\text{A}=2\text{a}$ and let O be the mid point of A´A. On adding (i) and (ii) we get

$\text{SA }\,+\,\,\text{S}{{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\,\,=\,\,\text{e}\left( \text{AZ}\,\,+\,\,{{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\text{Z} \right)\,\,\Rightarrow \,\,2\text{a}\,\,=\,\,\text{e}(\text{OZ}\,\,-\,\,\text{a}\,\,+\,\,\text{a}\,\text{ }+\,\,\text{OZ})$

$\Rightarrow \,\,2\text{a}\,\,=\,\,2\text{eOZ}\,\,\Rightarrow \,\,\text{OZ}\,\,=\frac{\text{a}}{\text{e}}$ ...(iii)

Again, subtracting (i) from (ii)

$\text{S}{{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\,\,-\,\,\text{SA}=\,\,\text{e}\,\,\left( {{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\text{Z}\,\,-\,\,\text{AZ} \right)\,\,\Rightarrow \,\,(\text{SO}\,\,+\,\,\text{a})\,\,-\,\,(\text{a}-\,\,\text{SO})\,\,=\,\,\text{e}\left( {{\text{A}}^{\prime }}\text{A} \right)$

$\Rightarrow \,\,2\text{SO}\,\,=\,\,\text{e}(2\text{a})\,\,\Rightarrow \,\,\text{OS}\,\,=\,\,\text{ae}$ ...(iv)

Let us take $\text{O}$ as origin, $\text{{A}'A}$ as $\text{x}$-axis and a line $\bot $ to $\text{{A}'A}$ and passing through $\text{O}$ as $\text{y}$ axis then point $\left( \text{ae},0 \right)$ and $\text{Z}$ is the point $\left( \frac{\text{a}}{\text{e}},0 \right)$. If $\text{P}\left( \text{x},\text{y} \right)$ be any point on the ellipse then $\text{PS}=\text{ePM}$ where $\text{PM}$ is the perpendicular from $\text{P}$ on directrix $\text{{V}'V}\text{.}$

$\Rightarrow \text{P}{{\text{S}}^{2}}={{\text{e}}^{2}}\text{P}{{\text{M}}^{2}}\Rightarrow {{(\text{x}-\text{ae})}^{2}}+{{\text{y}}^{2}}={{\text{e}}^{2}}{{\left( \text{x}-\frac{\text{a}}{\text{e}} \right)}^{2}}$ ...(v)

$\Rightarrow {{\text{x}}^{2}}\left( 1-{{\text{e}}^{2}} \right)+{{\text{y}}^{2}}={{\text{a}}^{2}}\left( 1-{{\text{e}}^{2}} \right)\Rightarrow \frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}\left( 1-{{\text{e}}^{2}} \right)}=1$ ...(vi)

On putting ${{b}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}\left( 1\,\,-{{e}^{2}} \right)$ we get the required standard equation $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ which is an ellipse

(a) Centre is origin

(b) Focus is at (ae, 0)

(c) Directrix is the line $x=\frac{a}{e}$.

The equation to the ellipse, whose focus and directrix are any given point and line, and whose eccentricity is known, is easily written down.

For example, if the focus be the point $\left( -2,3 \right)$, the directrix be the line $2x+3y+4=0$, and the eccentricity be $\frac{4}{5}$, the required equation is

${{(\text{x}+2)}^{2}}\,\,+\,\,{{(\text{y}-3)}^{2}}\,\,={{\left( \frac{4}{5} \right)}^{2}}\frac{{{(2\text{x}+3\text{y}+4)}^{2}}}{{{2}^{2}}+{{3}^{2}}}$

i.e., $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }261{{\text{x}}^{2}}+181{{\text{y}}^{2}}-192\text{xy}+1044\text{x}-2334\text{y}+3969\,\,=\,\,0$

Generally, the equation to the ellipse, whose focus is the point ( $\text{f},\text{g}$ ), whose directrix is $\text{Ax}+\text{By}$ $+\text{C}=0$, and whose eccentricity is $\text{c}$, is

${{(x-f)}^{2}}+{{(y-g)}^{2}}={{e}^{2}}\frac{{{(Ax+By+C)}^{2}}}{{{A}^{2}}+{{B}^{2}}}$

Second focus and second directrix of the ellipse 

On the negative side of origin take a point ${S}'$ which is such that

$CS=\text{C}{{\text{S}}^{'}}=ae$

and another point $\text{{Z}'}$ then

$CZ=C{{Z}^{'}}=\frac{a}{e}$

$\therefore$ Co-ordinate of ${S}'$ are $\left( -\text{ae},0 \right)$ and equation of second directrix $(i.e.,{{\text{Z}}^{\prime }}{{\text{M}}^{\prime }})$ is

$\text{x}=-\frac{\text{a}}{\text{e}}$

Let $\text{P}\left( \text{x},\text{y} \right)$ be any point on the ellipse then

$\text{{S}'P}=\text{eP}{{\text{M}}^{'}}$

or ${{\left( \text{{S}'}\text{P} \right)}^{2}}={{\text{e}}^{2}}{{\left( \text{P}{{\text{M}}^{'}} \right)}^{2}}$

or ${{(x+ae)}^{2}}+{{(y-0)}^{2}}={{e}^{2}}{{\left( x+\frac{a}{e} \right)}^{2}}$

or ${{(\text{x}+\text{ae})}^{2}}+{{\text{y}}^{2}}={{(\text{ex}+\text{a})}^{2}}$

or ${{x}^{2}}\left( 1-{{\text{e}}^{2}} \right)+{{\text{y}}^{2}}={{\text{a}}^{2}}\left( 1-{{\text{e}}^{2}} \right)$

or $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}\left( 1-{{e}^{2}} \right)}=1$

or $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1,\text{ where }{{b}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}\left( 1-e \right)$

The equation being the same as that of the ellipse when S(ae, 0) is focus and MZ i.e., x = a/e is directrix.

Hence co-ordinates of foci are (± ae, 0) and equations of directrices are x = ± a/e.

Note : 1. Distance between foci SS′ = 2ae and distance between directrices ZZ′ = 2a/ e

2. If e = 0

then ${{b}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}(1-0)$

∴ ${{b}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}$

then equation of ellipse $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1$ changes in circle

i.e, ${{x}^{2}}+{{y}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}$
